# IRON / MLIR-AIE

> IRON is a close-to-metal Python API for programming AMD Ryzen™ AI NPUs,
> built on an open-source MLIR-based compiler toolchain (mlir-aie).

IRON lets performance engineers write Python code that compiles directly to
AMD AI Engine (AIE) hardware — the NPU inside Ryzen AI processors. It
exposes tile placement, data movement via ObjectFifos, and host runtime
as Python objects, and compiles them to an xclbin + instruction stream via
the LLVM/MLIR-based Peano compiler.

## Key concepts

- **Worker**: code running on one AIE compute tile
- **ObjectFifo**: streaming data channel between tiles or host↔tile
- **Runtime**: host-side sequence — fill inputs, drain outputs
- **TensorAccessPattern (TAP)**: describes multi-dimensional DMA tiling
- **@iron.jit**: decorator that JIT-compiles a design to the NPU on first call

## Documentation sections

- [Getting Started](https://xilinx.github.io/mlir-aie/): install IRON on Ubuntu, build and run your first design
- [Programming Guide](https://xilinx.github.io/mlir-aie/programming_guide/README/): full tutorial from tiles to multi-core ML examples
- [API & Internals](https://xilinx.github.io/mlir-aie/api/): Python API (iron, taplib, kernels) and MLIR/C++ internals
- [MLIR Dialect Reference](https://xilinx.github.io/mlir-aie/AIEDesignPatterns/): AIE, AIEX, AIEVec dialect and pass docs
- [C++ API](https://xilinx.github.io/mlir-aie/doxygen/html/): Doxygen reference for the compiler internals

## Repository

- GitHub: https://github.com/Xilinx/mlir-aie
- License: Apache-2.0 WITH LLVM-exception
- Wheels: pip install mlir_aie (Python 3.11–3.14, Linux/Windows)

## Target hardware

AMD Ryzen™ AI (Phoenix, Hawk Point, Strix Point) — XDNA™ and XDNA™ 2 NPUs.
Targets AMD Ryzen™ AI NPUs (npu1: Phoenix/HawkPoint, npu2: Strix/Krackan).
